title: Testing CLI Applications
content:
Test CLI tools at multiple levels: unit tests for individual functions, integration tests that run the actual binary, and snapshot tests for output verification.

The assert_cmd crate lets you run your binary as a subprocess and check its exit code, stdout, and stderr. The predicates crate provides flexible assertions.

By separating logic into lib.rs, you can unit test business logic without spawning processes. Integration tests in tests/ verify the full CLI experience.
keyPoints: Separate logic into lib.rs for easy unit testing
Use assert_cmd to test the actual binary as a subprocess
Test exit codes, stdout, and stderr
Use tempfile for temporary test files that auto-cleanup
Test both success and error cases (missing files, bad input)
Integration tests in tests/ directory run against the compiled binary
